page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the N2 family. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.8 GHz, utilizing hyper-threading with 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the average tier across multiple workloads. In CPU-focused tests, it achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1408.79 and a multi-core score of 14822.17, alongside a Geekbench multi-core score of 8666.0. Memory subsystem performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 818.42 GB/sec, which drops to 96.6 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write bandwidth of 55.45 GB/sec.

For specialized tasks, the CPU architecture supports AVX, AVX-512 (including AVX-512 VNNI), and AES cryptography instructions. These hardware capabilities enable a Passmark CPU extended instructions score of 13066.17 and an encryption score of 6852.82. In database and serving benchmarks, the instance handles 14.1 million Redis SET operations per second and achieves a static web serving throughput of 12.55 GB/sec. Large language model inference using a small 135M model reaches 600.67 tokens/sec for prompt processing, though performance drops significantly to a weak 20.71 tokens/sec when processing prompts on a medium 7B model.

This instance offers a balanced resource density for memory-intensive applications without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware. It is well-suited for workloads such as database operations, memory-cached web serving, software compilation, and light text or image processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz. With 8 physical cores and 2 threads per core, it delivers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not bundle local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results place its performance in the average tier, with a Passmark CPU score of 15630.45 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 8666.0. It demonstrates solid memory bandwidth, reaching 818.42 GB/sec for cached small blocks, and handles 14.1 million Redis SET operations per second. This hardware profile is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering balanced mid-tier performance for databases and memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-16 is an Intel-based memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, built for database and memory-heavy tasks.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated vCPUs and a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz
